skill.name: twitter-automation
skill.description: Automate Twitter/X actions through the inference.sh CLI, including posting, media posts, likes, reposts, direct messages, follows, profile lookup, and post deletion.
skill.summary: Run Twitter/X publishing and engagement workflows with inference.sh CLI apps.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: Static findings for Ruby or shell backtick execution are mostly false positives caused by Markdown code fences and inline command formatting. However, the skill includes a confirmed remote install script piped into sh and enables high-impact Twitter/X account actions through an external CLI, so publication should require human review and stronger safety guidance.

high_findings.title: Remote Install Script Piped To Shell
high_findings.description: The quick start runs a remote installer with curl and pipes it directly to sh before login. This is a true positive because it executes network-delivered code without requiring manual inspection first.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 17 directly pipes content from https://cli.inference.sh into sh. The surrounding install note claims checksum verification, but the execution pattern remains a recognized supply-chain risk.

high_findings.title: High-Impact Social Account Automation
high_findings.description: The skill can post tweets, create media posts, like posts, retweet, delete posts, send direct messages, and follow users. These actions are legitimate for the skill purpose but can affect a user's public account, reputation, and private communications.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The listed apps and examples clearly include account-mutating Twitter/X actions. This is intended functionality, but it creates high impact if prompts or inputs are misused.

medium_findings.title: External CLI Execution Is Core Functionality
medium_findings.description: The allowed tool declaration permits Bash execution for infsh commands, and the examples call inference.sh apps with user-controlled input values. This is expected behavior, but it depends on the external CLI and remote service handling inputs safely.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill intentionally invokes infsh from Bash and passes structured user content to remote apps. The command scope is narrower than arbitrary shell access, so the risk is moderate outside the install command.

medium_findings.title: Network-Dependent Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill references inference.sh, cloud-hosted media, installer URLs, documentation URLs, and remote media URLs. Network access is required for its purpose, but it should be disclosed because content and account actions leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The network URLs are explicit and central to the workflow. No evidence found of hidden exfiltration, but user content is sent to external services by design.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: The many reported Ruby or shell backtick findings map to Markdown code fences, inline app identifiers, and command examples. No evidence found that SKILL.md contains executable Ruby backtick syntax.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file is Markdown, and the backticks delimit code samples or inline command names. There is no Ruby source file or runtime context in the scanned file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The scanner reported weak cryptographic algorithm findings, but the visible cryptographic reference is SHA-256 checksum verification. No evidence found of MD5, SHA-1, or custom weak cryptography in the scanned file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 23 describes SHA-256 verification, which is not a weak hash for checksum integrity. The other reported lines do not show cryptographic implementation evidence.

low_findings.title: Local File Writes In Examples
low_findings.description: Some examples save sample input or redirect generated media metadata into local JSON files. These writes are limited to relative filenames shown in documentation and do not target sensitive paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file writes are explicit in the examples and use relative filenames. They are minor because no sensitive read path, overwrite target, or persistence behavior is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Pipe Remote Script Into Shell
dangerous_patterns.description: The installer command downloads a remote script and executes it immediately. Users should prefer manual download and checksum verification before execution.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact shell pipeline is present on one line and has direct execution semantics.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Account Mutation Commands
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented apps perform public and private Twitter/X account changes, including posting, deleting, liking, reposting, sending DMs, and following users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The table and examples explicitly identify these account actions. The risk is semantic rather than hidden.

content.value_statement: Manual social posting and engagement can slow down content operations. This skill provides promptable inference.sh CLI workflows for Twitter/X posts, media, likes, reposts, DMs, follows, and lookups.
actual_capabilities: Posts text updates to Twitter/X through the x/post-tweet app.
Creates media posts with a text field and a media URL.
Likes, retweets, deletes, and retrieves posts by tweet ID.
Sends direct messages when given a recipient ID and message text.
Follows users and retrieves user profiles by username.
Combines generated image or video outputs with a follow-up social post.
limitations: Requires the inference.sh CLI, login, and a configured Twitter/X integration.
Does not include built-in rate limit management or campaign analytics.
Can only act within the permissions granted to the connected account.
Remote service calls may publish content or send messages immediately.

use_cases.title: Launch Updates
use_cases.description: Prepare and publish short product announcements from a connected Twitter/X account.

use_cases.title: Creator Media Posts
use_cases.description: Generate image or video assets with inference.sh apps and publish them as media posts.

use_cases.title: Community Engagement
use_cases.description: Like, repost, follow, and reply through controlled workflows after reviewing target IDs.

prompt_templates.title: Post A Simple Update
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft a concise Twitter/X update for this announcement, then show the infsh command to post it after my approval: [announcement].

prompt_templates.title: Create A Media Post
prompt_templates.scenario: Post with an existing media URL
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Twitter/X media post using this message and media URL. Keep the post under the platform limit and ask before running it: [message], [media URL].

prompt_templates.title: Review Engagement Actions
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these tweet IDs and prepare safe infsh commands to like or repost only the approved items: [tweet IDs and notes].

prompt_templates.title: Build A Launch Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a launch workflow with a generated image, a primary Twitter/X post, and follow-up engagement checks. Include each command and the approval point before execution.

output_examples.input: A user asks for a short post announcing a new feature.
output_examples.output: A concise post draft, a clear reminder to review the text, and the matching inference.sh posting command.

output_examples.input: A user provides a media URL and asks for a Twitter/X post.
output_examples.output: A polished caption, the media post command, and a note that running it may publish immediately.

output_examples.input: A user provides tweet IDs for engagement review.
output_examples.output: A short action list that separates likes, reposts, and items that should be skipped.

best_practices: Review every post, DM, follow, and delete action before running a command.
Use manual installation and checksum verification instead of piping remote scripts into sh.
Keep account credentials and connected integrations limited to the minimum required permissions.
anti_patterns: Do not auto-run commands that publish content without explicit user approval.
Do not use the skill for spam, impersonation, harassment, or artificial engagement.
Do not paste private customer data, secrets, or unreleased material into social post inputs.

faq.question: Does this skill post directly to Twitter/X?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented infsh commands can publish or modify content when the account integration is configured.

faq.question: Does it require inference.sh?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflows depend on the inference.sh CLI and its Twitter/X app integrations.

faq.question: Can it send direct messages?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill includes an x/dm-send app example that sends a message to a recipient ID.

faq.question: Can it schedule posts?
faq.answer: The description mentions scheduling, but the file does not show a specific scheduling app or command.

faq.question: Is the install command risk-free?
faq.answer: No. The quick start pipes a remote installer into sh. Manual download and checksum verification are safer.

faq.question: Should commands run without review?
faq.answer: No. Users should approve all commands that post, delete, message, follow, like, or repost.
